verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich protein member of the CRISP family, expressed in the epididymis and secreted into the epididymal lumen. The NCBI summary indicates it is secreted and binds to sperm during the reproductive process. While canonically a secreted soluble protein without intrinsic membrane anchoring, CRISP1 and related CRISP family members have documented capacity to bind and remain associated with sperm surface proteins, particularly ion channels (e.g., CaV2.2), in a wash-resistant manner during sperm maturation and fertilization. This stable association with sperm plasma membrane targets qualifies as surface-attached biology. The surface accessibility is restricted to reproductive / germline lineages and the specific fertilization context, making this a contextual case of stable surface attachment via partner-mediated tethering in a tissue-restricted setting.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Precise wash-resistance and stability of CRISP1 binding to sperm-surface ion-channel targets during in vivo epididymal maturation vs. in vitro binding assays; degree of ecto-presentation on oocyte / somatic cell surfaces beyond sperm-restricted display is not well-established in the training context.
